document.title: Japan Test Report
authorityOrJurisdiction: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
standards: Ordinance Concerning Technical Regulations Conformity Certification etc. of Specified Radio Equipment in Annex 1
Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex "45" of Article 88, Paragraph 1

sourceSummary.oneLine: Japan radio test report JR740701-11AN Rev. 01 for Laird Connectivity ST60-2230C 802.11ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module variants.
sourceSummary.officialUse: Supports regulatory compliance evidence for Japanese specified radio equipment requirements under Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19-3 and ARIB STD-T71.
keyPoints: The report is a supplementary report to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AN.
The tested representative model was ST60-2230C.

Summary test results list Pass for antenna power, frequency tolerance, E.I.R.P, burst length, unwanted emission strength, adjacent channel emitted power, occupied bandwidth, secondary radiated emissions, interference prevention function, and carrier sense.
The report was issued as Rev. 01 on Feb. 25, 2021.

entities.name: International Certification Corp.
entities.evidence: We, International Certification Corp., would like to declare that the tested sample has been evaluated and in compliance with the requirement of the above standards.

entities.name: Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications
entities.evidence: the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex “45” of Article 88, Paragraph 1

evidence.claim: The document is Japan Test Report JR740701-11AN, Rev. 01.
evidence.evidenceText: Report No.: JR740701-11AN Page No. : 1 of 29 Report Version: Rev. 01 Japan Test Report

evidence.claim: The equipment under test is an 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module, model ST60-2230C.
evidence.evidenceText: Equipment : 802.11 ac/a/b/g/n M.2 2230 + Bluetooth 4.2 module ... Model No. : ST60-2230C

evidence.claim: The applicant is Laird Connectivity, Inc. and the brand name is Laird Connectivity.
evidence.evidenceText: Brand Name : Laird Connectivity Applicant : Laird Connectivity, Inc.

evidence.claim: The report was initially issued as Rev. 01 on Feb. 25, 2021.
evidence.evidenceText: Release Record Report No. Version Description Issued Date JR740701-11AN Rev. 01 Initial issue Feb. 25, 2021

evidence.claim: The report applies Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19-3.
The measurement method references the Ordinance Concerning Technical Regulations Conformity Certification, MIC notification Annex 45 of Article 88 Paragraph 1, and ARIB STD-T71.
evidence.evidenceText: Measurement was conducted by the following test method: the test method of Ordinance Concerning Technical Regulations Conformity Certification etc. of Specified Radio Equipment in Annex 1, the Ministry of Internal Affairs and Communications notification in Annex “45” of Article 88, Paragraph 1 and ARIB STD-T71

evidence.claim: The report is supplementary to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AN.
evidence.evidenceText: This report is issued as a supplementary report to original ICC report no. JR740701-01AN.

evidence.claim: After verification, the listed changes did not impact the original test results.
evidence.evidenceText: After verification, above changes does not impact to original test results. Thus test results remain the same.

evidence.claim: Model ST60-2230C was selected as the representative model for final testing.
evidence.evidenceText: The above models, model ST60-2230C was selected as a representative one for the final test and only its data was recorded in this report.

evidence.claim: The EUT frequency ranges are 5150-5250 MHz, 5250-5350 MHz, and 5470-5730 MHz.

The summary of test results records Pass for antenna power, frequency tolerance, E.I.R.P, burst length, unwanted emission strength, adjacent channel emitted power, occupied bandwidth, secondary radiated emissions, interference prevention function, and carrier sense.
evidence.evidenceText: Summary of Test Results ... Antenna Power Pass ... Frequency Tolerance Pass ... E.I.R.P Pass ... Transmission Burst Length Pass ... Unwanted Emission Strength Pass ... Adjacent Channel Emitted Power Pass ... Occupied Bandwidth Pass ... Secondary Radiated Emissions Pass ... Interference Prevention Function Pass ... Carrier Sense Pass

evidence.claim: The test laboratory was International Certification Corp. at TH01-WS in Kwei Shan, Tao Yuan City, Taiwan.
evidence.evidenceText: Test Laboratory International Certification Corp. Test Site TH01-WS Address of Test Site (Kwei Shan) No. 3-1, Lane 6, Wen San 3rd St., Kwei Shan District, Tao Yuan City 333, Taiwan, R.O.C.

evidence.claim: The tested dates were Jun. 09 to Jun. 15, 2017.

reviewFlags.reason: The source is a test report, not a grant or certificate; regulatory approval status should not be inferred beyond the reported test compliance statements.
Authority is inferred from Japan, MIC notification text, and Article 2 Paragraph 1 Item 19-3; no separate issuing regulator or certificate authority is identified.
OCR/text extraction quality is uneven in appendices, including repeated blank appendix pages and malformed tokens such as Tnom/Tonm variants.
